name: Heal Palestine
mission: Sheltering Program provides immediate, practical protection for families who have nowhere else to go.
theoryOfChange: A holistic approach through four pillars (Health, Education, Aid, Leadership) to meet basic needs while empowering local leadership for long-term recovery.
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2024 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

evaluationSources: Palestinian Medical Council supervision of exams

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

keyConcerns.headline: 84% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions "high financial efficiency" but doesn't specify the high domestic burn rate (84% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations) which is a key risk factor and likely contributed to the risk deduction of -5.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a strong score due to its high financial efficiency and direct humanitarian impact in an urgent crisis zone. Its Zakat eligibility and low fundraising costs make it a highly aligned choice for humanitarian-focused donors.
areas_for_improvement: High cost per beneficiary relative to large-scale international NGOs
Limited long-term financial history due to the recent nature of the 2025 filings

dimension_explanations.alignment: The charity is highly aligned with donors seeking urgent humanitarian intervention and is officially Zakat-eligible [2]. It addresses critical needs such as food security and medical support, evidenced by its health advisory board's active engagement with injured patients [6][7].
dimension_explanations.impact: HEAL Palestine demonstrates high impact through direct service delivery, including operating bakeries and sheltering programs for families with nowhere else to go [4][5]. The organization maintains a strong financial position with 17.8 months of working capital and a program expense ratio of 88.2%, reflecting a commitment to directing resources toward its core mission [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: HEAL Palestine Inc. provides critical humanitarian relief through high-impact sheltering, food distribution, and medical support for displaced families.
strengths: Exceptional fundraising efficiency with only $0.01 spent to raise every $1
Strong liquidity with 17.8 months of working capital available
High program expense ratio of 88.2% dedicated to direct aid
Verified Zakat-eligible status for Muslim donors
baseline_narrative.summary: HEAL Palestine Inc. is a humanitarian organization dedicated to providing immediate protection and essential aid to families in Palestine who have lost their homes [4]. The organization reported a total revenue of $20,223,260 and maintains a high program expense ratio of 88.2%, ensuring the vast majority of funds directly support its bakery, sheltering, and food parcel programs [1][3].

case_against.summary: The primary limitation is the <cite id="15">lack of a long-term track record</cite>, as the organization was founded in 2024. There is also a <cite id="13">high domestic burn rate</cite> relative to international grants.

confidence.notes: Strong transparency via Candid Platinum seal compensates for short operational history.

alignment.explanation: There is a strong fit for Muslim donors as the charity <cite id="10">accepts zakat</cite> and serves categories such as <cite id="19">the poor, needy, and wayfarers</cite> in Muslim-majority regions.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this charity due to its <cite id="9">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> and clear <cite id="10">zakat-eligible status</cite>. The board of <cite id="16">six members</cite> provides adequate oversight for an organization of this size.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity should <cite id="14">document a clear theory of change</cite> with an explicit causal pathway for its long-term programs.

impact.explanation: Operations are highly direct, with an <cite id="17">average cost of $399.96 per beneficiary</cite> for global health and aid services. This is supported by <cite id="8">eight tracked outcome metrics</cite>.
impact.improvement: The organization could improve impact by <cite id="18">expanding services to underserved geographies</cite> with limited existing nonprofit coverage.

rich_narrative.headline: HEAL Palestine Inc. provides critical humanitarian relief through high-impact sheltering, food distribution, and medical support for displaced families.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to provide immediate humanitarian relief to displaced Palestinian families through a highly efficient, zakat-eligible organization.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat obligations for the crisis in Gaza
Value low fundraising overhead and high program ratios
Seek to support a specialized organization focused exclusively on Palestinian needs
giving_considerations: The organization is in its early growth stage (founded 2024)
High liquidity suggests the charity is well-positioned for long-term projects
Focus is on direct aid rather than long-term advocacy
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking organizations with decades of historical financial data or those focused on domestic US poverty.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified outcome tracking with <cite id="8">8 metrics reported to Candid</cite>, but without independent RCT evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The organization provides immediate sheltering and medical aid to stabilize families, creating a foundation for future education and leadership development.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking metrics ensures that food and shelter programs are reaching the intended number of displaced families effectively.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The massive scale of displacement in Gaza creates a significant funding gap that exceeds current revenue.

peer_comparison.differentiator: HEAL Palestine matches the <cite id="11">88.2% peer program ratio median</cite> while maintaining superior <cite id="7">fundraising efficiency of $0.01 per dollar</cite>.

strengths.detail: The organization spends only <cite id="7">$0.01 for every $1 raised</cite>, ensuring donor funds are not consumed by high marketing costs.
A significant <cite id="4">88.2% of total expenses</cite> are dedicated to direct program services like sheltering and medical aid.
The charity maintains <cite id="6">17.8 months of operating liquidity</cite>, which is critical for navigating the unpredictable access challenges in Gaza.

strengths_deep_dive: HEAL Palestine's operational model prioritizes direct service delivery in high-conflict zones. By maintaining a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 88.2%</cite>, they exceed the <cite id="11">industry standard of 75%</cite> for humanitarian organizations. Their strategic focus on <cite id="12">sheltering and food parcels</cite> addresses the most immediate survival needs of displaced families. Furthermore, the organization has achieved <cite id="9">Candid's Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite>, indicating a commitment to sharing detailed progress and results with the public.
rich_narrative.summary: HEAL Palestine Inc. is a humanitarian organization dedicated to providing <cite id="1">immediate, practical protection for families</cite> who have lost their homes in the Gaza Strip and surrounding regions. Founded in <cite id="2">2024</cite>, the charity focuses on four pillars: Health, Education, Aid, and Leadership. Despite its recent founding, the organization has quickly scaled its operations, reporting <cite id="3">$20,223,260 in total revenue</cite> to address the urgent humanitarian crisis. The charity operates with high financial efficiency, maintaining a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 88.2%</cite>. This ensures that the vast majority of donor contributions are directed toward <cite id="5">sheltering, food distribution, and medical support</cite>. HEAL Palestine also demonstrates strong fiscal management with <cite id="6">17.8 months of working capital</cite> in reserve, providing a significant buffer for sustained operations in a volatile environment. Their fundraising model is exceptionally lean, spending only <cite id="7">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite> in public support. Beyond emergency aid, the organization tracks <cite id="8">eight distinct outcome metrics</cite> to monitor the effectiveness of its health and aid programs. This data-driven approach is reflected in their <cite id="9">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level of disclosure available. For Muslim donors, the organization provides a <cite id="10">verified zakat-eligible pathway</cite>, serving multiple asnaf categories including the poor and needy.
